title: Extend apps/e2e with commerce-owner flows + seed fixtures

tasks.title: Fix e2e seed: logueable commerce owner owning gastronomy + experience, profile_completed=true
tasks.description:
In apps/e2e/seeds/e2e-seed.ts, ensure there is at least one logueable commerce owner (e.g. gastro-owner-1@hospeda.test or a dedicated e2e user) who:
- Has a real password so Better Auth sign-in works (not a placeholder).
- Owns at least one gastronomy listing AND one experience listing.
- Has profile_completed = true so the profile-completion middleware gate does not intercept before the commerce area.

If the current seed assigns experience ownership to commerce-owner-seed@hospeda.test (no login credential), re-assign ownership to the logueable user or create a new logueable seed user.

Note: also check packages/seed (dev seed) — if the same gastro-owner-*@local.test users seed with profile_completed=false there, note it as a follow-up but do NOT scope that fix here (this task is e2e-seed.ts only).

Key files:
- apps/e2e/seeds/e2e-seed.ts (primary)

Acceptance: after running `e2e:seed`, a query against the e2e DB confirms the logueable user exists with profile_completed=true and owns rows in both gastronomies and experiences tables. Sign-in via `fetch ${API_URL}/api/auth/sign-in/email` returns a session for this user.

subtasks.title: Reuse logueable gastro-owner-julieta@local.test; reassign experience ownership from non-logueable commerce-owner-seed@hospeda.test (fix in packages/seed/example, which e2e-seed reuses via example:true)
Set profileCompleted=true on commerce owners (insert + idempotent heal UPDATE) in packages/seed/example/gastronomies.seed.ts
Logueable credential confirmed in code (accounts row, providerId=credential, bcrypt Password123!); runtime sign-in validated by the T-003 E2E

tasks.title: Add reusable commerce-owner sign-in helper to apps/e2e/fixtures/
tasks.description:
Add a thin `signInAsCommerceOwner()` helper (or extend apps/e2e/fixtures/api-helpers.ts) reusing the existing Better Auth fetch pattern:
  fetch(`${API_URL}/api/auth/sign-in/email`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ email, password }) })

This helper should:
- Accept optional email/password overrides (defaulting to the seed user from T-001).
- Return the session cookie/token needed for authenticated Playwright requests.
- Mirror the existing signupUser / forceVerifyEmail / refreshSession pattern in api-helpers.ts.

Only add this helper if it is consumed by 2+ test files. If only one test file uses it, inline the sign-in directly in the spec (to avoid premature abstraction).

Key files:
- apps/e2e/fixtures/api-helpers.ts (extend) OR
- apps/e2e/fixtures/commerce-helpers.ts (new, if commerce-specific shape warrants it)

Acceptance: helper is importable from the fixture barrel; a quick smoke in the test verifies the returned session is valid (getMe returns COMMERCE_OWNER role).

subtasks.title: Decided: extend api-helpers.ts (signInExistingUser) — no separate commerce-helpers.ts
Implemented signInExistingUser() using the existing Better Auth fetch sign-in pattern

tasks.title: E2E positive spec: owner edits gastronomy + experience, changes reflected on public ficha
tasks.description:
Create apps/e2e/tests/commerce/commerce-01-owner-self-service.spec.ts.

Use apps/e2e/tests/host/host-01-onboarding-handoff.spec.ts as the structural template: JSDoc header (Actors, Tags, Preconditions, What it validates, @see), tags @p0 @commerce.

Spec must cover:
1. Commerce owner signs in (via helper from T-002 or inline sign-in).
2. Navigates to /[lang]/mi-cuenta/comercio — sees ONLY their own listings (gastronomy + experience from seed T-001).
3. Opens the gastronomy editor, edits one operational field (e.g. priceRange or contactInfo.phone), saves, asserts success.
4. Verifies the edited field appears on the public ficha (/[lang]/gastronomia/[slug]).
5. Repeats steps 3-4 for the experience listing.

Key files:
- apps/e2e/tests/commerce/commerce-01-owner-self-service.spec.ts (NEW)

Acceptance: `pnpm --filter hospeda-e2e e2e:test:p0` picks up and runs this spec; all assertions pass against a seeded e2e environment.

subtasks.title: Scaffolded commerce-01-owner-edits-listings.spec.ts with JSDoc header + @p0 @commerce tags
Implemented gastronomy editor edit (menuUrl) + public ficha verify
Implemented experience editor edit (richDescription) + public ficha verify

tasks.title: E2E negative spec: tourist/non-owner blocked from commerce editor
tasks.description:
Add a second test block to apps/e2e/tests/commerce/commerce-01-owner-self-service.spec.ts (or a companion file) covering the negative cases:

1. A tourist (non-commerce-owner) who navigates directly to /[lang]/mi-cuenta/comercio is redirected (to signin or mi-cuenta home — assert the final URL is NOT the comercio page).
2. A different commerce owner who navigates to another owner's editor URL (/mi-cuenta/comercio/[vertical]/[other-owner-id]/editar) receives a 404 or redirect (NOT the editor UI).

Precondition: two distinct seed users must be available — one owner (from T-001), one tourist (should already exist in e2e-seed.ts).

Key files:
- apps/e2e/tests/commerce/commerce-01-owner-self-service.spec.ts (extend)

Acceptance: both negative cases pass; the assertions are tagged @p0 @commerce and run in the existing CI e2e:test:p0 grep.

subtasks.title: Tourist redirect: navigate to comercio, assert final URL is /es/mi-cuenta/ (real guard)
Cross-owner: Julieta opens Rodrigo's editor, assert redirect to her /es/mi-cuenta/comercio/ + editor form not visible (real guard is redirect, not 404)

tasks.title: Verify CI picks up @p0 commerce tests + document in spec
tasks.description:
Confirm that the @p0 tag on the new commerce tests is sufficient for the existing CI to pick them up — no new workflow file or script needed.

Steps:
1. Locally run `pnpm --filter hospeda-e2e e2e:test:p0` and verify the commerce-01 spec appears in the test run output.
2. Inspect .github/workflows/e2e-pr.yml: confirm the `--grep @p0` (or equivalent) pattern covers any test tagged @p0, regardless of directory.
3. Update spec.md section 6 (Tasks) to reflect all tasks as done, and add a note under AC-2 with the verified grep command.
4. If any gap is found (e.g. directory filter that would miss tests/commerce/), fix it — but this should be a one-line config change at most.

Key files:
- .github/workflows/e2e-pr.yml (read-only verify, fix only if needed)
- .qtm/specs/SPEC-252-web-e2e-playwright-harness/spec.md (note the verified command)

Acceptance: running e2e:test:p0 locally lists and runs the commerce-01 spec; the CI grep pattern is confirmed to be directory-agnostic.

subtasks.title: Verified e2e:test:p0 = 'playwright test --grep @p0' and testDir './tests' includes tests/commerce/ (local run deferred to CI, web prod-env build blocker)
Confirmed e2e-pr.yml runs e2e:test:p0; grep is directory-agnostic (both commerce specs tagged @p0)
Documented the implementation + verified CI command in spec.md Revision History
